name: Small Plane (simpleFoam)
description: External aerodynamics for a light aircraft in freestream flow. Uses snappyHexMesh for body-fitted meshing and simpleFoam for steady-state RANS simulation. No ground plane — the geometry floats in open air with slip conditions on all domain boundaries.
learning_objectives: Copy and prepare surface geometry (STL/OBJ format)
Refine mesh around geometry with snappyHexMesh
Configure freestream boundary conditions (slip on all domain faces)
Post-process lift and drag coefficients with forceCoeffs

mesh.title: Generate Mesh
mesh.description: Three-step meshing: surfaceFeatureExtract detects sharp edges, blockMesh creates the background hex mesh, then snappyHexMesh refines around the aircraft geometry. The domain should extend far enough in all directions to avoid blockage effects.
mesh.tip: For aircraft, the domain should extend at least 10 chord lengths upstream, 20 downstream, and 10 in all lateral directions to minimise far-field boundary influence.

boundaries.title: Set Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Configure freestream boundary conditions. All domain faces (top, bottom, front, back) use slip — there is no ground plane. Only the aircraft surfaces use no-slip wall conditions.
boundaries.tip: Unlike ground vehicle cases, lowerWall uses slip (not wall) because the aircraft is in freestream. The inlet fixes velocity at 30 m/s and the outlet uses inletOutlet.

solver.title: Configure Solver
solver.description: Set up simpleFoam with SIMPLE algorithm. This is a steady-state solver — endTime controls the maximum number of iterations.
solver.tip: For a quick test run, reduce endTime to 200 iterations. Full convergence typically needs 500+ iterations for aircraft with complex wake and tip vortices.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. Watch residuals — all fields should decrease below 1e-4 for convergence. Monitor lift and drag coefficients for convergence.
run.tip: Lift coefficient (Cl) may take 300+ iterations to stabilise. Check both residuals and Cl/Cd convergence before stopping.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualise pressure distribution on the aircraft surface, velocity field around the wing, and wake structure. Check forceCoeffs output for lift and drag values (positive Cl = upward lift).
postprocess.tip: In ParaView: apply 'Extract Block' to select aircraft surfaces, then colour by pressure coefficient. Use a cutting plane at y=0 to visualise the symmetry plane flow field and check for separation.
